mechanism_narrative: A declining or persistently low FEDFUNDS level reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically supporting valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and 20-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of falling FEDFUNDS show no significant excess return versus periods of rising FEDFUNDS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals an incipient labor-market deterioration that leads investors to reprice corporate earnings downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and stagnation in RSAFS indicates a joint housing and consumer-spending slowdown that jointly signals weakening aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month; neither series alone is sufficient because housing weakness without retail softening is often absorbed by substitution effects.
regime_hint: housing downturn with soft consumer spending
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to show negative average 20-day returns distinct from single-series triggers, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating INDPRO growth (industrial output momentum slowing but still positive) signals capacity-utilization exhaustion that foreshadows margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; in early or mid-cycle regimes, the same deceleration is benign and the effect reverses or disappears.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month annualized growth decelerates below 2% while still positive
failure_condition: The negative-return relationship fails to hold specifically in late-cycle windows (identified via low UNRATE/high PAYEMS growth history), or reverses sign in early/mid-cycle windows less than expected, i.e. regime split does not produce a significant direction difference (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in PAYEMS growth reflects strengthening labor demand and consumer income, which supports near-term risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are not significantly different from returns following negative surprises (p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 3%, it raises the probability of monetary tightening and increases discount-rate uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as equity risk premia widen.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle or tightening risk elevated
failure_condition: Triggered episodes (CPI YoY > 3%) show no significant negative excess 20-day return relative to non-triggered periods (p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth combined with accelerating RSAFS retail sales jointly confirm a durable aggregate-demand expansion, reinforcing earnings growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; GDPC1 alone is too lagged and noisy, so confirmation from RSAFS is required to activate the signal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 3%
failure_condition: The joint-trigger positive return effect is not statistically distinguishable from the effect of GDPC1 alone (interaction coefficient p > 0.10), or triggered episodes underperform the unconditional 60-day mean return.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-downturn regime, a further month-over-month decline in HOUST intensifies fears of a broader demand contraction and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week; in a housing-recovery or stable regime, the same decline is noise and has no reliable directional effect.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline while trailing 12-month HOUST level is below its 2-year moving average
regime_hint: housing downturn regime (HOUST below its 2-year trend)
failure_condition: Negative 5-day return effect fails to differ significantly between housing-downturn and housing-recovery regimes (regime interaction p > 0.10), or triggered downturn-regime episodes show positive average returns.
